content:
Watch the video below to learn Grammarly's process of writing winning essays!



After you finish this page, click Next to continue to How to Prewrite for Your Assignment.

Week 1 | How to Prewrite for Your Assignment
Before the Words Flow
Now that you understand the basics of a Narrative Essay, it's time to take the first step in writing an excellent essay: Prewriting!

Prewriting is the part of the writing process where you generate ideas for writing. Writing thoughts on paper or typing phrases in a Word document primes your brain for writing. Many different prewriting strategies work well.

Freewriting
Freewriting is just what it sounds like—you write freely whatever comes into your head. The idea here is to keep your fingers moving. Time yourself for 5 minutes, and keep writing even if you make a typo or if you're writing, "I don't know what to write." The uninterrupted flow allows the writer to tap into their subconscious during this exercise. Set the freewriting aside and do another freewriting after a few minutes. Often, you can identify recurring ideas when you do a series of four or five freewritings. A recurring notion might be fruitful to develop into a more extended piece of writing.
